name: Lora Brodys Bete Noire
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound (227g) sweet butter, at room temperature, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Place the rack in the center of the oven.
2. Butter a 9-inch (23 cm) cake pan that is not a springform and line the bottom with a circle of parchment or wax paper, covering it completely. Butter the paper.
3. Chop both chocolates into fine pieces and set aside.
4. In a heavy-bottomed saucepan, combine water and 1 cup sugar. Bring to a rapid boil over high heat. Remove from heat and add chocolate pieces, stirring until melted. Add butter, piece by piece, stirring to melt it completely.
5. In the bowl of an electric mixer or a regular mixing bowl, beat eggs and 1/3 cup sugar until they have tripled in volume for a crunchy crust, or mix only until sugar dissolves for a smooth top.
6. Gradually add the chocolate/butter mixture to the eggs, stirring to incorporate completely.
7. Do not overbeat! This causes air bubbles that ruin the texture.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and place it in the oven with a sturdy jelly-roll pan filled with hot water. Bake for 25-30 minutes.
9. Let cool in the pan for 10 minutes, then run a sharp knife around the sides to release the cake. Cover with plastic wrap and transfer onto a cookie sheet. Invert a serving plate over the cake and flip it so the plate is on top and the cake below.
10. Serve with either a chocolate ganache glaze or a white chocolate sauce.
Chocolate Ganache:
1 cup heavy cream
10 ounces (113g) bittersweet chocolate, chopped
Heat the cream until just before boiling. Remove from heat and add chocolate, stirring gently until smooth. Allow to cool slightly before pouring over the cake.
White Chocolate Sauce:
1 cup heavy cream
9 ounces (255g) Tobler Narcisse, chopped
1/2 Drambuie (optional)
Heat the cream until just before boiling. Remove from heat and add the chopped chocolate. If it does not melt completely, process in a food processor or blender for a few seconds. Add Drambuie if desired.
